description: Continuous Emission Monitoring System (CEMS) requirements including QA/QC procedures, data availability, and reporting obligations for cement manufacturing.

typical_cement_kiln_cems.method: 40 CFR 60 Appendix B, PS-2 (UV fluorescence or NDIR)
40 CFR 60 Appendix B, PS-4A (NDIR or GFC)
40 CFR 60 Appendix B, PS-18 (FTIR or laser)
40 CFR 60 Appendix B, PS-1 (opacity) or PS-11 (PM CEMS)
40 CFR 60 Appendix B, PS-3 (paramagnetic or NDIR)
40 CFR 60 Appendix B, PS-6 (differential pressure, ultrasonic, or thermal)

installation_requirements.location: Representative location in stack/duct per 40 CFR 60.13
installation_requirements.access: Safe and permanent access platforms required for all CEMS probes
installation_requirements.sample_transport: Heated sample lines maintained above dewpoint to prevent condensation

RATA.frequency: Annual (or semi-annual if previous RATA failed criteria)
RATA.procedure: Compare CEMS readings to EPA reference method results over 9+ test runs

acceptance_criteria.bias_adjustment: Apply bias adjustment factor if RA passes but bias test fails

RATA.consequences_of_failure: Invalidates CEMS data, triggers corrective action and retest

CGA.procedure: Challenge analyzers with EPA protocol gases at low, mid, and high ranges
CGA.notes: Can substitute CGA for one of the quarterly audits if RATA is done in that quarter

daily_calibration.procedure: Zero and span check using calibration gases

acceptance_criteria.action_if_exceeded: Adjust analyzer, recalibrate, investigate root cause

cylinder_gas_audits_linearity.acceptance: Within 5% of reference value at each level

data_availability.calculation: Valid data hours / Total unit operating hours x 100

substitute_data.description: When CEMS data is missing or invalid, substitute data procedures apply per 40 CFR 75 or permit conditions
substitute_data.penalty: Substitute data is typically conservative (high values), creating incentive to maintain high data availability

consequences_of_low_availability: Regulatory violation if below permit threshold
May trigger enforcement action or NOV

quarterly_reports.due_date: 30 days after end of quarter (varies by permit)

annual_reports.due_date: Per Title V permit schedule (typically 60-90 days after year end)

electronic_reporting.EPA_ECMPS: Electronic Continuous Monitoring Plan System for acid rain affected units
electronic_reporting.CEDRI: Compliance and Emissions Data Reporting Interface for NESHAP reporting
electronic_reporting.state_systems: Many states have additional electronic reporting requirements
